request: Set up a spacecraft in a 500 km circular low-Earth orbit at 51.6 degrees inclination, propagate for one day, and report the altitude and semi-major axis.
intent: A single spacecraft on a ~500 km circular LEO (SMA about 6878 km, ECC about 0, inclination 51.6 deg), propagated for about one day, with a ReportFile recording altitude and semi-major axis.

request: Propagate the ISS for 90 minutes and report its geodetic latitude and longitude.
intent: A spacecraft on an ISS-like LEO propagated for about 90 minutes (5400 s), with a ReportFile recording the spacecraft's latitude and longitude.

request: Place a spacecraft in a geostationary orbit and propagate it for one day.
intent: A spacecraft in a geostationary orbit: SMA about 42164 km, near-zero eccentricity, and near-zero inclination, propagated for about one day.

request: Set up a Molniya orbit and propagate it for 12 hours.
intent: A spacecraft on a Molniya orbit: highly eccentric (ECC about 0.74), critical inclination near 63.4 deg, argument of perigee near 270 deg, period about 12 hours, propagated for roughly half a day.

request: Propagate a 600 km circular orbit for exactly one orbital period.
intent: A ~600 km circular spacecraft propagated for one full orbital revolution, using a stop condition equivalent to one period (e.g. returning to periapsis/apoapsis or an elapsed time of about 5800 s).

request: Propagate an eccentric orbit to its next periapsis.
intent: An eccentric spacecraft propagated until the next periapsis passage, i.e. a Propagate command whose stop condition is the spacecraft's Periapsis.

request: Set up a spacecraft and write its six Keplerian orbital elements to a report file.
intent: A spacecraft whose six classical Keplerian elements (SMA, ECC, INC, RAAN, AOP, TA) are written to a ReportFile.

request: Visualize a low-Earth orbit in a 3D orbit view while propagating for one day.
intent: A LEO spacecraft propagated for about a day with an OrbitView resource displaying the trajectory in 3D.

request: Propagate a low-Earth orbit for one day and show its ground track.
intent: A LEO spacecraft propagated for about a day with a GroundTrackPlot resource showing the sub-satellite ground track.

request: Define a spacecraft by its Cartesian position and velocity vectors and propagate it for one hour.
intent: A spacecraft whose state is specified in Cartesian coordinates (X, Y, Z, VX, VY, VZ rather than Keplerian elements) and propagated for about one hour.

request: Set a spacecraft's epoch to 01 Jun 2026 00:00:00 UTC and propagate it for one day.
intent: A spacecraft whose initial epoch is set to 01 Jun 2026 in a UTC Gregorian date format, propagated for about one day.

request: Create a spacecraft with a dry mass of 850 kg in a 500 km circular orbit and propagate for one day.
intent: A ~500 km circular spacecraft whose dry mass is set to 850 kg, propagated for about one day.

request: Set up a circular polar orbit at 800 km altitude and propagate for one day.
intent: A near-circular spacecraft at about 800 km altitude (SMA about 7178 km) with an inclination near 90 degrees (polar), propagated for about one day.

request: Propagate a low-Earth orbit for three days.
intent: A LEO spacecraft propagated for about three days of elapsed time.

request: Report the apogee and perigee altitudes of an eccentric orbit.
intent: An eccentric spacecraft with a ReportFile recording its apoapsis and periapsis radii or altitudes (the apogee and perigee).

request: Plot a low-Earth orbit's altitude against time over one day.
intent: A LEO spacecraft propagated for about a day with an XYPlot of altitude on the Y axis versus elapsed time on the X axis.

request: Set up a circular equatorial orbit at 700 km altitude and propagate for one day.
intent: A near-circular spacecraft at about 700 km altitude (SMA about 7078 km) with an inclination of zero (equatorial), propagated for about one day.

request: Compute and report the orbital period of a 500 km circular orbit.
intent: A ~500 km circular spacecraft whose orbital period is written to a ReportFile.

request: Propagate a LEO for one day and write its position to a report file with column headers enabled.
intent: A LEO spacecraft propagated for about a day, with a ReportFile that has column headers turned on (WriteHeaders true) recording the spacecraft position.

request: Propagate a spacecraft for exactly 3600 seconds.
intent: A spacecraft propagated for one hour using an elapsed-seconds stop condition of about 3600 s.

request: Raise the apogee of a 500 km circular orbit using a single prograde impulsive burn.
intent: Starting from a ~500 km circular LEO, one prograde (along-velocity, +Element1 in a VNB burn) impulsive maneuver is applied that increases the orbit's apogee/energy.

request: Set up a sun-synchronous orbit at about 700 km altitude.
intent: A spacecraft in a sun-synchronous orbit at ~700 km altitude: SMA about 7078 km, near-circular, with the sun-synchronous retrograde inclination near 98 degrees.

request: Lower the perigee of a 600 km circular orbit with a single retrograde impulsive burn at apogee.
intent: Starting from a ~600 km circular orbit, one retrograde (against-velocity, -Element1 in VNB) impulsive maneuver applied near apogee that lowers the perigee.

request: Change a circular orbit's inclination by 10 degrees using a single out-of-plane impulsive burn.
intent: A circular spacecraft with one impulsive maneuver directed out of plane (an Element2/normal component in VNB, not purely along-track) that changes the orbital inclination by roughly 10 degrees.

request: Apply a finite burn to a spacecraft using a thruster and fuel tank, burning for 60 seconds.
intent: A spacecraft equipped with a fuel tank and a thruster, with a FiniteBurn turned on and then off around a propagation of about 60 seconds (BeginFiniteBurn ... Propagate ... EndFiniteBurn).

request: Propagate a 300 km low orbit for several days including atmospheric drag.
intent: A low (~300 km) spacecraft propagated for a few days with a force model that includes atmospheric drag (a drag/atmosphere model enabled), not just point-mass gravity.

request: Propagate a geostationary spacecraft for several days including solar radiation pressure.
intent: A GEO spacecraft propagated for several days with a force model that includes solar radiation pressure (SRP enabled), typically with the Sun as a point mass.

request: Propagate a high orbit with Earth gravity plus the Sun and Moon as point-mass perturbations.
intent: A high-altitude spacecraft propagated with a force model whose central body is Earth and that adds the Sun and the Moon (Luna) as point-mass perturbing bodies.

request: Propagate a low-Earth orbit using an 8 by 8 non-spherical Earth gravity field.
intent: A LEO spacecraft propagated with a force model using a non-spherical Earth gravity field of degree and order about 8 (a harmonic gravity model, not a point mass).

request: Define an Earth-centered Sun-pointing (VNB-style) coordinate system and report a spacecraft's state in it.
intent: A user-defined CoordinateSystem (a custom origin/axes, e.g. an object-referenced or VNB/LVLH frame) with the spacecraft's state reported relative to that frame.

request: Perform the first burn of a Hohmann transfer from a 500 km circular orbit to a higher circular orbit, then coast to apogee.
intent: Starting from a ~500 km circular orbit, a prograde impulsive maneuver at periapsis that raises apogee toward the target altitude, followed by a propagation/coast to apogee.

request: From an eccentric transfer orbit, circularize at apogee using a single prograde burn.
intent: Starting on an eccentric orbit, coast to apogee then apply a prograde impulsive maneuver sized to circularize the orbit at the apogee radius (raising perigee to apogee).

request: Define a ground station and report when a low-Earth orbit spacecraft is in view above a 10 degree elevation mask.
intent: A GroundStation on Earth with a minimum elevation around 10 degrees and a LEO spacecraft, propagated while reporting visibility/elevation contact between the station and the spacecraft.

request: Create a formation of two spacecraft in similar orbits and propagate them together.
intent: Two spacecraft grouped into a Formation resource and propagated together as the formation.

request: Set up a geostationary transfer orbit with perigee at 250 km and apogee at geostationary altitude.
intent: A spacecraft on a GTO: perigee altitude about 250 km and apogee near geostationary radius (about 42164 km), giving SMA near 24400 km and ECC near 0.73, typically at a low/launch inclination.

request: Using a For loop, propagate a spacecraft in one-hour steps for a full day, reporting its state each step.
intent: A spacecraft propagated inside a For loop that advances in ~1-hour increments across about 24 iterations, writing a report each iteration.

request: Propagate a Molniya orbit to apogee and report the apogee altitude.
intent: A Molniya spacecraft (high eccentricity, ~63.4 deg inclination) propagated to apoapsis, with a ReportFile recording the apogee altitude/radius.

request: Propagate a low-Earth orbit for one day using a high-accuracy RungeKutta89 integrator with a tight accuracy setting.
intent: A LEO spacecraft propagated for about a day with a propagator configured to use a RungeKutta89 (PrinceDormand78-class) integrator and a tight accuracy/error tolerance.

request: Use a differential corrector to target an apogee radius of 8000 km by varying an impulsive burn.
intent: A targeting sequence using a DifferentialCorrector that varies an impulsive burn and achieves an apogee radius (RMAG at apoapsis) of 8000 km.

request: Propagate the spacecraft and write its trajectory to a CCSDS-OEM ephemeris file.
intent: A spacecraft propagated with an EphemerisFile output whose file format is CCSDS-OEM.

request: Use a differential corrector to perform a full Hohmann transfer from LEO to geostationary altitude with two burns, achieving a final semi-major axis of 42164 km.
intent: A targeting sequence with two impulsive burns that achieves a final circular orbit at geostationary radius (SMA about 42164 km): a first burn raising apogee and a second circularizing, with the DifferentialCorrector achieving the final SMA/RMAG of ~42164 km.

request: Use a differential corrector to drive a spacecraft's inclination to zero by varying an out-of-plane burn.
intent: A targeting sequence that varies an out-of-plane impulsive burn and achieves a final inclination of 0 degrees (a plane change to the equator).

request: Use an optimizer to minimize the total delta-v of a two-burn orbit raise to a target altitude.
intent: An optimization sequence (Optimize ... EndOptimize) that varies the burn magnitudes of a two-impulse transfer and minimizes the total delta-v / fuel cost subject to reaching the target orbit.

request: Propagate the spacecraft and write its trajectory to a SPICE SPK ephemeris file.
intent: A spacecraft propagated with an EphemerisFile output whose file format is SPK (a SPICE .bsp kernel).

request: Propagate the spacecraft and export its trajectory in STK ephemeris (.e) format.
intent: A spacecraft propagated with an EphemerisFile output whose file format is the STK time-position-velocity ephemeris (STK-TimePosVel / .e).

request: Use a differential corrector to perform an east-west station-keeping burn that returns a geostationary spacecraft to its target longitude drift.
intent: A targeting sequence on a geostationary spacecraft that varies an along-track impulsive burn to achieve a near-zero longitude drift rate (semi-major axis back at geostationary radius), i.e. east-west station keeping.

request: Set up a spacecraft near the Sun-Earth L2 libration point and report its state in a libration-point coordinate system.
intent: A spacecraft positioned near the Sun-Earth L2 point, using a LibrationPoint resource (primary Sun, secondary Earth, point L2) and a coordinate system referenced to it, with the state reported in that frame.

request: Propagate a trans-lunar trajectory from a high Earth orbit to lunar periapsis with the Moon included in the force model.
intent: A spacecraft on a translunar trajectory propagated with a force model that includes the Moon (Luna) as a point mass/primary, stopping near lunar periapsis (closest approach to the Moon).

request: Use a differential corrector to target B-plane parameters BdotT and BdotR at a planetary flyby by varying a burn.
intent: A targeting sequence that varies an impulsive burn and achieves specified B-plane coordinates (BdotT and BdotR) at a flyby, using a DifferentialCorrector.

request: Use a differential corrector to target a final semi-major axis by varying the duration of a finite burn.
intent: A targeting sequence that turns on a finite burn (thruster + tank), varies the burn duration or an associated parameter, and achieves a target semi-major axis with a DifferentialCorrector.

request: Propagate a spacecraft on a heliocentric cruise in a Sun-centered force model out to about 1 AU of travel.
intent: A spacecraft propagated in a Sun-centered (heliocentric) force model with the Sun as the central/primary body, cruising on an interplanetary arc rather than orbiting Earth.
